checks.a_analysis: The output contains headings and list items that correspond directly to the content found in the source document. There is no evidence of invented text, hallucinated conclusions, or fabricated sections.

The output does not contain any fabricated phone numbers or URLs. While several URLs and the 'Join meeting' link from the source are missing in the output, no incorrect or invented contact information has been added.

All main sections defined by headings (Welcome, Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Regular Status Updates, Other Business) are present in the output. While sub-content within these sections is missing (addressed in C5), no entire primary section or the single page was dropped.
The source document contains lists and a small text block for meetings, but no formal tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are widespread omissions of sub-bullet points across almost every section of the document. The output fails to capture secondary list items that provide critical detail. Notable omissions include sub-bullets for 'Elevation', 'Administrative/Governmental Units', 'NC Board on Geographic Names', 'Metadata Committee', and 'Other Business'. Additionally, the text 'Join meeting' under Web Conference and the entire document footer containing website URLs are missing. The cumulative loss of content across these sections exceeds the 25% threshold.
checks.b_evidence_source: Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”; County Boundaries - Gary Thompson; Municipal Boundaries – Sarah Wray; SMAC Discussion and Approval; Simone Mountain; Stiles Mountain; Update on refresh of documents/tools on NC OneMap (www.nconemap.gov/pages/metadata); Adjustment to reflect the correct statutes and bring bylaws in line with those of other subcommittees; SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov; Join meeting
checks.b_evidence_output: [These items are entirely absent from the corresponding sections in the output JSON]
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Other Business, Web Conference paragraph, and Document Footer.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the first bullet point of the 'Welcome/Introductions' section, the word 'minutes' is omitted. While the context implies what is being approved, the loss of the noun changes the literal content of the agenda item.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON does not contain any duplicated text blocks from the source document. Each section and list item appears exactly once as intended in the source.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows the source document's structure. H1 is used for the main title, and H2 for the primary agenda sections. H3 is used appropriately for the '2021 Meetings' sidebar information. No levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. The '2021 Meetings' sidebar is correctly represented as a section with a heading and paragraph rather than being forced into a table structure.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the agenda. The '2021 Meetings' sidebar, which is positioned to the right of the 'Working Groups' section in the source, is placed after the Working Groups section in the JSON, which is a standard and acceptable linearized reading order.

The list conversion failed to capture nested list structures. In multiple sections, sub-bullets present in the source are entirely omitted from the output. For example, under 'Elevation - John Lay', the sub-bullet 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”' is missing. Similarly, nested items under 'Administrative/Governmental Units', 'NC Board on Geographic Names', and 'Other Business' are gone. This results in significant loss of structural meaning and content.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Elevation – John Lay
• Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”

checks.a_analysis: Several hyperlinks present in the source are missing in the output. The text for 'Join meeting' and 'minutes' (which are links in the PDF) is missing from their respective paragraph and list elements. Additionally, the footer containing the SMAC and NC OneMap website URLs is completely absent from the JSON representation.
checks.b_evidence_source:

Web Conference: Join meeting

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to render the nested (indented) list items present in several sections of the source PDF. In the 'Framework+ Datasets' section, sub-bullets for 'Elevation' and 'Administrative/Governmental Units' (such as 'County Boundaries' and 'Municipal Boundaries') are completely missing from the visual layout. Similarly, in the 'Working Groups' section, the multi-level nested items under 'NC Board on Geographic Names' (SMAC Discussion, Simone Mountain, Stiles Mountain) and the sub-bullet under 'Metadata Committee' are absent. In 'Other Business', the sub-bullet regarding Bylaws adjustments is also missing. This results in a flattened and incomplete visual organization that does not match the source document's hierarchical intent. Additionally, the first item of the 'Framework+ Datasets' list ('Orthoimagery') is visually separated from the rest of the list items which follow it.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains nested list items under several headings, such as 'Administrative/Governmental Units' which has 'County Boundaries' and 'Municipal Boundaries' as sub-bullets, and 'NC Board on Geographic Names' which has three levels of nesting.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows flat lists where these nested items are entirely absent. For example, 'NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt' is immediately followed by 'Working Group for Municipal Boundaries - John Bridgers' with no intervening content.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Other Business.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; the layout consists of headings and bulleted lists. Therefore, there are no tables to misrender.

The source PDF consists entirely of text and horizontal divider lines. There are no meaningful images, logos, or charts that require rendering in the HTML. The divider lines are decorative and their absence or replacement with CSS styling is an expected visual difference.

There is no visual duplication of body content. The presence of document titles and section headings in both the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body is a standard accessibility feature and not considered content duplication.
